title: Odisha braces for prolonged heat spell; Yellow warning issued for multiple districts - Odisha TV
search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Odisha
full_text:
Odisha is set to witness a prolonged spell of dry weather over the next seven days, with the India Meteorological Department (IMD) issuing a Yellow Warning (Be Aware) for multiple districts due to rising temperatures and increasing humidity. As per the latest forecast, dry weather is very likely to prevail across all districts of the state from April 14 to April 20, with no significant rainfall expected during this period. This is likely to intensify heat conditions, especially in coastal and western regions.
Hot and humid conditions are expected to persist at isolated places in several coastal and adjoining districts throughout the forecast period. These include Balasore, Bhadrak, Jajpur, Kendrapara, Cuttack, Jagatsinghpur, Puri, Khordha, Nayagarh, Ganjam, and Gajapati. The combination of high humidity and rising temperatures is likely to cause considerable discomfort among residents, increasing the risk of heat-related illnesses such as dehydration and heat exhaustion.
From April 15 onwards, heatwave conditions are likely to develop in parts of western Odisha and gradually intensify over the following days. On April 15 and 16, heatwave conditions are expected at isolated places in Balangir, Kandhamal, Jharsuguda, and Sambalpur. The situation is likely to worsen from April 16 to 17, extending to Sonepur, Boudh, and Angul. By April 17 and 18, more districts including Nuapada and Nabarangpur are expected to come under the heatwave grip.
The intensity and spread of the heatwave are likely to increase further between April 18 and April 20, affecting a larger number of districts such as Sundargarh, Dhenkanal, Deogarh, Keonjhar, and Mayurbhanj, in addition to those already under its impact. The expanding heatwave conditions indicate a challenging week ahead for residents, particularly in western and interior parts of the state.
Authorities have advised people to take necessary precautions to avoid heat-related health issues. Residents are urged to avoid stepping out during peak afternoon hours, stay hydrated, wear light cotton clothing, and take special care of vulnerable groups such as children, the elderly, and outdoor workers. With no immediate relief in sight, the state is likely to experience a steady rise in discomfort levels as summer conditions intensify.
